english_text: We calculate a man's bride price partly based on the number of cattle his family owns.
hint: Say bride price is partly based on how many cattle the family has

english_text: The spotted cow always gives the most milk and the children named her after the first daughter.
hint: Say the best milk cow is named after the firstborn daughter

english_text: We spent a whole month building the new cattle pen from timber cut from the nearby forest.
hint: Say you spent a month building a new cattle pen from timber

english_text: The cattle tax must be paid each year and the chief comes to count the herd personally.
hint: Say the chief counts the herd each year to collect cattle tax

english_text: The wisdom of an old cattle herder is worth more than twenty books on animal keeping.
hint: Say an old herder's wisdom beats twenty books on animal care

english_text: A man who speaks soft words to his cattle will have calmer animals than a harsh man.
hint: Say cattle respond better to a man who speaks to them gently

english_text: We do not sell the grandmother cow because she is the memory of all the cattle before her.
hint: Say you never sell the oldest cow because she holds herd memory

english_text: You cannot buy what you have not seen, so always look at the animal before you pay.
hint: Say you must see an animal before paying for it

english_text: The elder who does not speak when he should is no better than a stone in the shade.
hint: Say a silent elder is as useless as a stone in the shade

english_text: Fire that is covered does not go out, it only waits for the right moment to burn bright.
hint: Say a hidden fire waits to burn — hidden strength returns

english_text: When the shepherd is absent, the wolves do not sleep or take a day off from hunting.
hint: Say trouble never rests when the protector is away

english_text: The person who digs the pit that their neighbor falls into will not escape it themselves.
hint: Say those who trap others will fall in their own trap

english_text: Water never flows uphill no matter how hard the wind pushes against the current.
hint: Say some things are impossible no matter how hard you try

english_text: The palm tree that stands alone in the field gets cut down first by the approaching storm.
hint: Say standing alone makes you vulnerable to danger

english_text: Knowledge is the only wealth a thief cannot take from you while you sleep at night.
hint: Say knowledge is the one wealth thieves cannot steal

english_text: The frog in the well does not know the size of the great ocean beyond his small world.
hint: Say someone who stays in one place doesn't know the wider world

english_text: A man who never makes a mistake never makes anything worth remembering at all.
hint: Say making mistakes is part of making something meaningful

english_text: The stick that was used to beat you today can be used to protect you tomorrow.
hint: Say what hurts you now can protect you later

english_text: You cannot wake up someone who is only pretending to be asleep in the afternoon.
hint: Say you cannot help someone who refuses to see the truth

english_text: The fish that opens its mouth too wide in the water is the one that the fisher hook catches.
hint: Say talking too much gets you into trouble

english_text: A person who tells all his secrets to his friend has given his friend a knife to use.
hint: Say telling all your secrets gives others power over you

english_text: Where the tree falls, that is where it rests, and life ends where God decided from the start.
hint: Say we end where God intended

english_text: Sweep the dust from your own door before telling your neighbor his compound is dirty.
hint: Say clean your own house before criticizing your neighbor's

english_text: When the cat is away the rats do not mourn, they eat the food and dance on the table.
hint: Say people misbehave when authority is absent

english_text: The calabash that travels far from home always comes back cracked and full of new stories.
hint: Say travelers return changed and with many stories

english_text: Greet the person at the door before you ask them what they want and why they came.
hint: Say greet first
column7: ask questions later — show hospitality first

english_text: A gift given in anger is not a gift, it is a burden wrapped up in nice cloth for the eye.
hint: Say a gift given in anger is not truly a gift

english_text: The one who carries the heavy load uphill does not brag about it when they reach the top.
hint: Say those who work hardest are often the most humble

english_text: If you want to know a man's character, give him power and then watch what he does.
hint: Say power reveals character — watch what someone does with it

english_text: The tree does not hate the axe because the axe does not know what it is doing.
hint: Say forgive those who hurt you — they often don't know better

english_text: Even the sweetest water from the deepest well will become bitter if you take too much.
hint: Say even the best things become bad when you take too much

english_text: A sleeping leopard is still more dangerous than a running rabbit if you are too close.
hint: Say a quiet danger can still be more dangerous than an active one

english_text: The young man who disrespects elders is building a house on sand at the ocean's edge.
hint: Say disrespecting elders is building your life on an unstable foundation

english_text: Do not count your harvest before the rain has stopped and the last crop is safely in.
hint: Say don't celebrate too early before everything is secured

english_text: The community that shares its problems has already halved them before speaking out loud.
hint: Say sharing problems with community already makes them smaller

english_text: A wise leader speaks last so he can hear everything before he decides on the right path.
hint: Say a wise leader speaks last after hearing everything

english_text: What the old woman says sitting still, the young man cannot see running up the mountain.
hint: Say elders have wisdom that youth cannot gain through activity

english_text: The goat does not know it is leading itself to the ceremony until it smells the smoke.
hint: Say some people don't realize where their choices are taking them

english_text: Walk softly in your neighbor's house so that your neighbor will walk softly in yours.
hint: Say treat others how you want to be treated in your own home

english_text: Anger is fire inside a house and patience is the water that keeps it from burning down.
hint: Say anger destroys but patience protects like water fights fire

english_text: The bird that builds its nest close to the ground is always the first to be disturbed.
hint: Say those who take shortcuts end up most vulnerable

english_text: Every mouth has its own taste and every person has their own reason for the things they do.
hint: Say everyone has their own perspective and reasons for their choices

english_text: Even the sun which gives light to all the world must bow and rest at the end of its day.
hint: Say even the greatest must rest — no one is above needing rest

english_text: A soldier's bravery is not shown in words but in what he does when the enemy arrives.
hint: Say bravery is shown in action not in words

english_text: The crocodile does not tell you it is dangerous, you know this already from what you see.
hint: Say some dangers announce themselves clearly without words

english_text: Never refuse a gift from an elder even if it is small because the thought is the treasure.
hint: Say never refuse an elder's gift however small — the thought is what counts

english_text: The ground does not forget the seeds that were planted in it even after many dry seasons.
hint: Say the ground remembers seeds — good deeds are never forgotten

english_text: A community that stops its traditions slowly loses the rope that holds all its people together.
hint: Say losing traditions weakens the bonds that hold a community

english_text: Tell me who walks with you and I will tell you where you are most likely going in this life.
hint: Say the company you keep shows where your life is headed

english_text: The termite builds its tall mound one grain of sand at a time over many patient seasons.
hint: Say big things are built slowly with patience one small step at a time

english_text: Freetown's cotton tree has stood at the center of town for centuries watching everything.
hint: Say Freetown's cotton tree has stood for centuries in the center

english_text: The baobab trees along the Gambia River are some of the oldest living things in West Africa.
hint: Say the Gambia River baobabs are among West Africa's oldest trees

english_text: The Sierra Leone River estuary is one of the widest and most beautiful in all of Africa.
hint: Say the Sierra Leone estuary is one of Africa's widest and most beautiful

english_text: The crossing from Banjul to Barra on the Gambia River takes about thirty minutes by ferry.
hint: Say the Banjul to Barra ferry crossing takes thirty minutes

english_text: The Fouta Djallon highlands in Guinea are the source of many great rivers in West Africa.
hint: Say the Fouta Djallon highlands are the source of many rivers

english_text: The border crossing at Rosso between Senegal and Mauritania is always busy and crowded.
hint: Say the Rosso border crossing between Senegal and Mauritania is always busy

english_text: Kolda in southern Senegal is surrounded by rice paddies and dense forest on all sides.
hint: Say Kolda is surrounded by rice paddies and dense forest

english_text: The old colonial buildings along Lumley Beach in Freetown have seen a hundred years pass.
hint: Say old colonial buildings on Lumley Beach have stood a century

english_text: The Mano River forms the border between Sierra Leone and Liberia in the south.
hint: Say the Mano River forms the Sierra Leone-Liberia border

english_text: The Niokolo-Koba national park in Senegal is home to lions, elephants, and hippos.
hint: Say Niokolo-Koba park in Senegal has lions

english_text: The great mosque in Touba in Senegal draws millions of pilgrims every year for the festival.
hint: Say Touba mosque in Senegal draws millions of pilgrims each year

english_text: Many Fula people from Guinea and Mali meet at the great cattle market in Diaobé.
hint: Say Fula people from Guinea and Mali meet at the Diaobé cattle market

english_text: The road that connects Freetown to the provinces is often the first to flood in heavy rain.
hint: Say the Freetown provincial road floods first in heavy rain

english_text: Conakry is a peninsula city surrounded by water on three sides and growing fast.
hint: Say Conakry is a peninsula city growing quickly

english_text: The Kola Peninsula area north of Freetown is known for its fine beaches and fishing villages.
hint: Say the Kola Peninsula has fine beaches and fishing villages

english_text: The border town of Gbalamuya connects Guinea and Sierra Leone through the forest road.
hint: Say Gbalamuya border town connects Guinea and Sierra Leone

english_text: My grandfather walked from Labé to Conakry twice a year trading cattle and buying goods.
hint: Say grandfather walked from Labé to Conakry twice yearly to trade

english_text: The Senegambia region has one of the densest concentrations of ancient stone circles in Africa.
hint: Say Senegambia has the densest ancient stone circles in Africa

english_text: The new deep water port at Freetown handles more shipping containers than ever before.
hint: Say Freetown's deep water port handles more containers than before

english_text: Kankan city on the Milo River in Guinea is an ancient trading and Islamic learning center.
hint: Say Kankan on the Milo River is an old trading and learning center

english_text: The Gambia River bisects the entire country from east to west providing water and transport.
hint: Say the Gambia River crosses the whole country east to west

english_text: The road from Ziguinchor to Kolda in Casamance passes through beautiful green forest.
hint: Say the Ziguinchor to Kolda road passes through green forest

english_text: We traveled by pirogue up the Casamance River to reach the village near the Senegal border.
hint: Say you traveled by pirogue up the Casamance River to a village

english_text: The Futa Jallon plateau in Guinea receives so much rain that it feeds rivers in six countries.
hint: Say the Futa Jallon plateau feeds rivers in six countries

english_text: Cape Sierra Leone at the northern tip of the peninsula is where ships first see the land.
hint: Say Cape Sierra Leone is the first land ships see

english_text: Kaolack on the Saloum River is Senegal's second busiest port and biggest peanut market.
hint: Say Kaolack is Senegal's second port and biggest peanut market

english_text: The Great Green Wall project is planting trees from Dakar all the way east to Djibouti.
hint: Say the Great Green Wall plants trees from Dakar to Djibouti

english_text: Koinadugu district in Sierra Leone has the highest altitude and coolest climate in the country.
hint: Say Koinadugu is Sierra Leone's highest and coolest district

english_text: We sailed from Banjul down the coast past the fishing village of Bakau in the afternoon.
hint: Say you sailed from Banjul past Bakau village in the afternoon

english_text: The ancient city of Kumbi Saleh in Mali was once the capital of the Ghana Empire long ago.
hint: Say Kumbi Saleh in Mali was once the ancient Ghana Empire capital

english_text: The Fouta Djallon herders bring their cattle down to the Gambia lowlands every dry season.
hint: Say Fouta Djallon herders bring cattle to Gambia lowlands in dry season

english_text: Timbuktu on the edge of the Sahara Desert was once the greatest center of learning in Africa.
hint: Say Timbuktu on the Sahara was once Africa's greatest learning center

english_text: The Atlantic Ocean coast of Sierra Leone stretches for over five hundred kilometers of beaches.
hint: Say Sierra Leone's Atlantic coast stretches five hundred kilometers

english_text: The iron ore mine at Tonkolili is one of the largest in all of West Africa by volume.
hint: Say the Tonkolili iron ore mine is one of West Africa's largest

english_text: The sacred Kankurang forest in Gambia is where young men go for their initiation ceremonies.
hint: Say the Kankurang forest is where Gambian young men go for initiation

english_text: Boundiali in northern Côte d'Ivoire is close to the border and shares much with Mandingo culture.
hint: Say Boundiali near the border shares Mandingo cultural ties

english_text: The coast road from Freetown to Tombo village is one of the most scenic in all of Sierra Leone.
hint: Say the Freetown to Tombo coast road is one of Sierra Leone's most scenic
